project.title: PERK crosstalk between ER and microtubule dynamics
project.projectDescription: The architecture of the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is tightly controlled as a key determinant of its function. Its dynamics are linked to those of the cytoskeleton, but our understanding of how this coordination occurs and what its functional relevance is, is limited. We found the Unfolded Protein Response (UPR) transducer EIF2AK3/PERK is essential for acute stress-induced peripheral redistribution and remodeling of the ER, through eIF2a phosphorylation and translation initiation shutdown. PERK-mediated eIF2a phosphorylation can be bypassed by blocking polysome assembly; by depleting microtubule-anchoring ER proteins such as REEP4, p180/RRBP1 and Climp63/CKAP4; or by disrupting the microtubule cytoskeleton. Notably, specific disruption of non-centrosomal microtubules, but not centrosome depletion, relieved blockade of ER redistribution in PERK-deficient cells. Conversely, PERK deficiency stabilized non-centrosomal microtubules promoting polarized protrusiveness in epithelial cells and neuroblasts. Thus, PERK coordinates ER architecture and homeostasis with cell morphogenesis by coupling ER remodeling and non-centrosomal MT stability and dynamics.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Cell plates were lysed with Lysis Buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.8, 2% SDS, 5 mM Tris-(2-Carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP)). Samples were boiled for 5 min and incubated for 20 min at room temperature for solubilization and reduction of the proteins, and after centrifugation at 15,000g for 15 min, supernatants were recovered, and protein concentration was determined by using a Direct Detect system (Millipore). The protein extracts were digested by the FASP method (Wiśniewski et al., 2009). For stable isobaric labelling, the resulting tryptic peptides were dissolved in 150 mM Triethylammonium bicarbonate (TEAB) buffer, and the peptide concentration was determined by measuring amide bonds with the Direct Detect system (Millipore). Equal amounts of each peptide sample were labelled using the TMTp 18plex reagents (Thermo Fisher) according to manufacturer's protocol with minor changes. Peptides were labelled with 0.6 mg of TMTp reagents previously reconstituted with 70 μL of acetonitrile, and after incubation at room temperature for 1h, reaction was stopped with 0.5% TFA, incubated for 10 min, and peptides were combined. Samples were concentrated in a Speed Vac, and removal of excess labelling reagents and desalting was accomplished onto Oasis-HLB columns, and dried-down. Another aliquot of this mixture was separated for the analysis of the full proteome. For increasing the proteome coverage, an aliquot was fractionated by high-pH reverse phase chromatography (High pH Reversed-Phase Peptide Fractionation Kit, Pierce) following the supplier’s protocol. The so-obtained peptide fractions were vacuum-dried and kept frozen for later LC-MS/MS analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Database search. For peptide identification the MS/MS spectra were searched with the Sequest HT algorithm (Tabb, 2015) implemented in Proteome Discoverer 2.5 (Thermo Fisher Scientific; Orsburn 2021) against the Uniprot database containing all human sequences (UniProt_June 2022), concatenated with decoy sequences generated using DecoyPyrat (Wright and Choudhary, 2016). The parameters were selected as follows: trypsin digestion with 2 maximum missed cleavage sites, precursor and fragment mass tolerances of 2 Da and 0.03 Da, respectively, carbamidomethyl (+57.021) cysteine and TMT modifications at peptide N-terminal and Lys residues as fixed modifications, and methionine oxidation (+15.995), proline hydroxylation (+15.995), protein N-terminus acetylation (+42.011) and Serine/Threonine phosphorylation (+79.096) were selected as dynamic modifications. The precursor charge range was set to 2-4.

references.referenceLine: Sánchez-Álvarez M, Lolo FN, Sailem H, Fulgoni G, Pascual-Vargas P, Agüera L, Catalá-Montoro M, Arias-García M, López JA, Vázquez J, Del Pozo MÁ, Bakal C. PERK-dependent reciprocal crosstalk between ER and non-centrosomal microtubules coordinates ER architecture and cell shape. Cell Rep. 2025 44(5):115590

Comment[Instrument]: Easy-NanoLC 1200 system (Thermo Scientific) coupled to a Tribrid Orbitrap Fusion mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific)

Comment[Separation]: separated online on a NanoViper PepMap100 C18 LC analytical column (75 μm ID, 50 cm, Thermo Scientific)
Comment[GradientTime]: continuous gradient of 5–32% B over 240 min and 32–90% B over 5 min
Comment[FlowRateChromatogram]: at a flow rate of 200 nL/min
Comment[FractionationMethod]: fractionated by high-pH reverse-phase chromatography using the High pH Reversed-Phase Peptide Fractionation Kit (Pierce)
